title: Couture, Romans of the Decadence
description: Test your knowledge on this painting.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What critique did Couture level against the French in *Romans of the Decadence*?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/68b974918b80cdef5ae46be8814eee36.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The contrast of the decadent figures in the painting's foreground to the heroic statues in back, Couture suggests a parallel in contemporary French society, which he feels has forgotten the values of the French Revolution in a search for pleasure and luxury.  

**[Watch this video to review.](/humanities/becoming-modern/avant-garde-france/second-empire/v/thomas-couture-romans-of-the-decadence-1847)**

choices.content: They have lost the values of the French Revolution and are slipping into decadence.
They no longer value the established artistic traditions of the Classical past.
They have rejected the timeless values of antiquity in favor of modernism.
They should reject bourgeois decadence and support the working classes.

assessment_items.question_content:
**All of the following are characteristic of French academic painting, as seen in Couture's *Romans of the Decadence*, EXCEPT . . .**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d70492110face070e79f14bd6781caac.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Couture used traditional techniques of perspective to render the space beyond the columns. Flattened forms and a shallow space are characteristic of the avant-garde movements of Realism and Impressionism.

choices.content: flattened forms and shallow space
the emphasis on the center of the composition
the idealized beauty of the reclining women.
the classical architectural surroundings

assessment_items.question_content:
**What term best describes Thomas Couture's *Romans of the Decadence*?**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d70492110face070e79f14bd6781caac.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Academic painting of the 19th century emphasized moral themes and classical artistic traditions rooted in ancient Greece and Rome.

choices.content: academic
modernist
classical
Second Empire

assessment_items.question_content:
**What distinguishes Couture's *Romans of the Decadence* from other French Academic painting of the period?  **

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
While Couture's painting exemplifies classical style of the French Academy, his decadent subject matter differs from the tradition of paintings to embody ideals of heroism and moral virtue.   

**[Watch this video to review.](/humanities/becoming-modern/avant-garde-france/second-empire/v/thomas-couture-romans-of-the-decadence-1847)**

choices.content: The emphasis on decadence and moral decay
The use of Classical subject matter
The clarity and order of the composition
The monumental scale and life-sized figures

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which formal devices in this painting contribute to the interpretation that past ideals have been forgotten?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a7dc352448f13b1b1d1c0ff0a39ee3fd.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Couture uses all these devices to suggest the past has been forgotten. He contrasts brightly colored and dramatically lit figures with the Classical architecture and sculpture that fades into the background as if into history. This contrast is reinforced by the sensuous bodies densely grouped in the foreground against the geometric clarity of the architecture and the even spacing of the columns.

**[Watch this video to review.](/humanities/becoming-modern/avant-garde-france/second-empire/v/thomas-couture-romans-of-the-decadence-1847)**

choices.content: The figures are more brightly colored than the background elements.
The contrasts of geometric and curving lines.
The use of light and shadow throughout the composition.
The crowded figure groupings in the foreground.
